Class Catalogue

java.lang.Object
Catalogue
public class Catalogue extends Object
Core in-memory catalogue that stores and manages a collection of Publication objects.

Architecture note: Catalogue acts as the Model layer of the application. It holds no UI state and knows nothing about Swing. All mutating methods operate directly on the internal ArrayList; the UI layer (BookGUI) calls these methods and then refreshes the table from the returned lists.

Persistence: The catalogue is serialised as a whole to a binary file via Java's built-in ObjectOutputStream. Because the stored graph consists only of Publication subclasses that implement Serializable, no external libraries are required.

Typical workflow

Catalogue cat = new Catalogue();
cat.addPublication(new Book("Dune", 1965, "Frank Herbert", "Chilton", "Sci-Fi"));
List<Publication> results = cat.searchByTitle("dune");
cat.saveToFile("my_catalogue.dat");
See Also:

  • Book

  • Constructor Details

    • Catalogue

      public Catalogue()
      Constructs a new empty Catalogue.

  • Method Details

    • addPublication

      public void addPublication(Publication p)
      Adds a publication to the catalogue.
      Parameters:
      p - the publication to add; must not be null

    • removePublicationByTitle

      public void removePublicationByTitle(String title) throws BookNotFoundException
      Removes the publication whose title exactly matches (case-insensitive) the given string.
      Parameters:
      title - the title to search for; must not be null
      Throws:
      BookNotFoundException - if no publication with that title exists

    • findPublicationByTitle

      public Publication findPublicationByTitle(String title) throws BookNotFoundException
      Finds and returns the first publication whose title exactly matches the given string (case-insensitive comparison).
      Parameters:
      title - the exact title to look for; must not be null
      Returns:
      the matching Publication; never null
      Throws:
      BookNotFoundException - if no matching publication is found

    • getAllPublications

      public List<Publication> getAllPublications()
      Returns a defensive copy of all publications currently in the catalogue.

      Modifications to the returned list do not affect the catalogue.

      Returns:
      a new List containing all publications; never null

    • searchByTitle

      public List<Publication> searchByTitle(String query)
      Returns all publications whose title contains the given query string (case-insensitive substring match).
      Parameters:
      query - the search string; must not be null
      Returns:
      a list of matching publications; empty if none found

    • searchByAuthor

      public List<Publication> searchByAuthor(String query)
      Returns all Book entries whose author field contains the given query string (case-insensitive substring match).

      Non-Book publications are ignored because they do not have an author field.

      Parameters:
      query - the search string; must not be null
      Returns:
      a list of matching books; empty if none found

    • filterByGenre

      public List<Publication> filterByGenre(String genre)
      Returns all Book entries that belong to the specified genre (case-insensitive exact match).
      Parameters:
      genre - the genre to filter by; must not be null
      Returns:
      a list of books in that genre; empty if none found

    • countUniqueAuthors

      public long countUniqueAuthors()
      Counts the number of distinct author names among all Book entries.
      Returns:
      the count of unique author strings; 0 if there are no books

    • countByGenre

      public Map<String,Long> countByGenre()
      Returns a map from genre name to the count of books in that genre.

      The map preserves insertion order (genres appear in the same order as returned by getDistinctGenres()).

      Returns:
      a Map where keys are genre names and values are counts; never null

    • getDistinctGenres

      public List<String> getDistinctGenres()
      Returns a sorted list of all distinct genre values present in the catalogue.

      Blank or null genre values are excluded. The result is sorted alphabetically for consistent display in UI components.

      Returns:
      a sorted, deduplicated list of genre strings; never null

    • exportToCSV

      public void exportToCSV(String filename) throws IOException
      Exports all Book entries to a CSV file with a header row.

      Each field is quoted to handle commas within values. The output format is: 

      Title,Author,Publisher,Year,Genre

      Non-Book publications are silently skipped.

      Parameters:
      filename - the path of the CSV file to create or overwrite
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while writing the file

    • saveToFile

      public void saveToFile(String filename) throws IOException
      Serialises the current publication list to a binary file using Java object serialisation.

      The file can later be restored with loadFromFile(String).

      Parameters:
      filename - the path of the file to write
      Throws:
      IOException - if the file cannot be created or written

    • loadFromFile

      public void loadFromFile(String filename) throws IOException, ClassNotFoundException
      Deserialises the publication list from a previously saved binary file.

      The existing in-memory catalogue is replaced entirely by the loaded data.

      Parameters:
      filename - the path of the file to read
      Throws:
      IOException - if the file cannot be read
      ClassNotFoundException - if the serialised classes are not on the classpath
